139	# -----------------------------------------------------------
140	# HCR tight-bbox cache
141	# -----------------------------------------------------------
142	def compute_tight_hcr_bboxes(
143	    sid: str,
144	    hcr_ids,
145	    force: bool = False,
146	    verbose: bool = True,
147	) -> pd.DataFrame:
148	    """Cache-backed per-cell tight HCR bboxes in level-0 voxel indices.
149	
150	    Parameters
151	    ----------
152	    sid : str
153	        Subject id (must be a spot subject with `metrics.pickle`).
154	    hcr_ids : iterable of int
155	        Cell label ids of interest. Cached entries are reused; missing
156	        ids are computed by reading the zarr crop covering the pickle
157	        tile bbox and masking `segmentation_mask == hcr_id`.
158	    force : bool
159	        Re-compute every id even if cached.
160	
161	    Returns
162	    -------
163	    pd.DataFrame
164	        Columns: ``hcr_id, zmin_vox, zmax_vox, ymin_vox, ymax_vox,
165	        xmin_vox, xmax_vox, volume_vox, zc_vox, yc_vox, xc_vox``
166	        restricted to the requested `hcr_ids` (see README in the cache
167	        directory for the schema).
168	    """
169	    want = {int(x) for x in hcr_ids}
170	    cache_path = _tight_bbox_cache_path(sid)
171	    if cache_path.exists() and not force:
172	        cached = pd.read_parquet(cache_path)
173	    else:
174	        cached = pd.DataFrame(columns=[
175	            "hcr_id", "zmin_vox", "ymin_vox", "xmin_vox",
176	            "zmax_vox", "ymax_vox", "xmax_vox",
177	            "volume_vox", "zc_vox", "yc_vox", "xc_vox",
178	        ])
179	
180	    have = set(int(x) for x in cached["hcr_id"].values)
181	    missing = sorted(want - have)
182	
183	    if missing:
184	        if verbose:
185	            print(f"  [{sid}] tight-bbox: {len(have & want)} cached, "
186	                  f"{len(missing)} to compute")
187	        with open(_hcr_metrics_path(sid), "rb") as f:
188	            metrics = pickle.load(f)
189	        z = zarr.open(str(_hcr_seg_zarr_path(sid)))["0"]
190	        new_rows = []
191	        t0 = time.time()
192	        for i, hid in enumerate(missing, 1):
193	            m = metrics.get(hid)
194	            if m is None:
195	                continue
196	            bb = np.asarray(m["global_bbox"], dtype=int)
197	            zlo, ylo, xlo, zhi, yhi, xhi = bb
198	            # pickle bbox endpoints are inclusive (verified with hid=10317);
199	            # read +1 to include the last slice
200	            crop = np.asarray(z[0, 0, zlo:zhi + 1, ylo:yhi + 1, xlo:xhi + 1])
201	            mask = (crop == hid)
202	            if not mask.any():
203	                continue
204	            zs, ys, xs = np.where(mask)
205	            new_rows.append({
206	                "hcr_id": int(hid),
207	                "zmin_vox": int(zs.min() + zlo),
208	                "zmax_vox": int(zs.max() + zlo + 1),
209	                "ymin_vox": int(ys.min() + ylo),
210	                "ymax_vox": int(ys.max() + ylo + 1),
211	                "xmin_vox": int(xs.min() + xlo),
212	                "xmax_vox": int(xs.max() + xlo + 1),
213	                "volume_vox": int(mask.sum()),
214	                "zc_vox": float(zs.mean() + zlo),
215	                "yc_vox": float(ys.mean() + ylo),
216	                "xc_vox": float(xs.mean() + xlo),
217	            })
218	            if verbose and (i % 200 == 0 or i == len(missing)):
219	                dt = time.time() - t0
220	                print(f"    {i}/{len(missing)}  ({i/max(dt,1e-3):.1f} cells/s)")
221	        new_df = pd.DataFrame(new_rows)
222	        if not cached.empty:
223	            combined = pd.concat([cached, new_df], ignore_index=True)
224	            combined = combined.drop_duplicates("hcr_id", keep="last")
225	        else:
226	            combined = new_df
227	        combined = combined.sort_values("hcr_id").reset_index(drop=True)
228	        combined.to_parquet(cache_path, index=False)
229	        cached = combined
230	
231	    return cached[cached["hcr_id"].isin(want)].reset_index(drop=True)
232	
233	
234	# -----------------------------------------------------------
235	# per-cell bbox tables
236	# -----------------------------------------------------------
237	def cz_cell_tight_bboxes(sid: str, s: SubjectData, cz_surf: dict) -> pd.DataFrame:
238	    """Per-CZ-cell tight xy-bbox area and pia depth."""
239	    mask = tifffile.imread(_cz_seg_mask_path(sid))
240	    rp = regionprops(mask)
241	    dz_um, dy_um, dx_um = s.cz_z_um, s.cz_xy_um, s.cz_xy_um
242	    rows = []
243	    for r in rp:
244	        zlo, ylo, xlo, zhi, yhi, xhi = r.bbox
245	        dz = (zhi - zlo) * dz_um
246	        dy = (yhi - ylo) * dy_um
247	        dx = (xhi - xlo) * dx_um
248	        zc, yc, xc = r.centroid
249	        xyz_um = cz_px_to_um(np.array([[zc, yc, xc]]), s)[0]
250	        x_um, y_um, z_um_c = xyz_um[2], xyz_um[1], xyz_um[0]
251	        depth = float(depth_from_surface(np.array([[x_um, y_um, z_um_c]]), cz_surf)[0])
252	        rows.append({
253	            "cz_id": r.label,
254	            "xy_area_um2": dx * dy,
255	            "bbox_dx_um": dx, "bbox_dy_um": dy, "bbox_dz_um": dz,
256	            "volume_vox": r.area,
257	            "volume_um3": r.area * dz_um * dy_um * dx_um,
258	            "x_um": x_um, "y_um": y_um, "z_um": z_um_c,
259	            "depth_um": depth,
260	        })
261	    return pd.DataFrame(rows)
262	
263	
264	def hcr_cell_tight_bboxes(
265	    sid: str,
266	    s: SubjectData,
267	    hcr_surf: dict,
268	    hcr_ids,
269	) -> pd.DataFrame:
270	    """Per-HCR-cell tight xy-bbox area and pia depth for the given ids.
271	
272	    Uses `segmentation_mask.zarr` (level-0) via the tight-bbox cache.
273	    Applies `s.hcr_seg_xy_um` for µm conversion and rescales voxel
274	    centroids to the level-2 frame before `hcr_px_to_um`.
275	    """
276	    with open(_hcr_metrics_path(sid), "rb") as f:
277	        metrics_keys = set(pickle.load(f).keys())
278	    want = {int(i) for i in hcr_ids if int(i) in metrics_keys}
279	    if not want:
280	        return pd.DataFrame()
281	    tb = compute_tight_hcr_bboxes(sid, want)
282	    if tb.empty:
283	        return pd.DataFrame()
284	
285	    seg_xy_um = s.hcr_seg_xy_um
286	    seg_z_um = s.hcr_seg_z_um
287	    dx_vox = (tb["xmax_vox"] - tb["xmin_vox"]).to_numpy(float)
288	    dy_vox = (tb["ymax_vox"] - tb["ymin_vox"]).to_numpy(float)
289	    dz_vox = (tb["zmax_vox"] - tb["zmin_vox"]).to_numpy(float)
290	    xy_area_um2 = (dx_vox * seg_xy_um) * (dy_vox * seg_xy_um)
291	
292	    zyx_px = tb[["zc_vox", "yc_vox", "xc_vox"]].to_numpy(float).copy()
293	    zyx_px[:, 1] /= HCR_SEG_XY_DOWNSAMPLE
294	    zyx_px[:, 2] /= HCR_SEG_XY_DOWNSAMPLE
295	    xyz_um = hcr_px_to_um(zyx_px, s)[:, [2, 1, 0]]
296	    depths = depth_from_surface(xyz_um, hcr_surf)
297	    return pd.DataFrame({
298	        "hcr_id": tb["hcr_id"].to_numpy(),
299	        "xy_area_um2": xy_area_um2,
300	        "bbox_dx_um": dx_vox * seg_xy_um,
301	        "bbox_dy_um": dy_vox * seg_xy_um,
302	        "bbox_dz_um": dz_vox * seg_z_um,
303	        "volume_vox": tb["volume_vox"].to_numpy(),
304	        "volume_um3": tb["volume_vox"].to_numpy() * seg_z_um * seg_xy_um * seg_xy_um,
305	        "x_um": xyz_um[:, 0], "y_um": xyz_um[:, 1], "z_um": xyz_um[:, 2],
306	        "depth_um": depths,
307	    })
308	
309	
310	# -----------------------------------------------------------
311	# driver
312	# -----------------------------------------------------------
313	def _prefilter_center_fov(
314	    sid: str, s: SubjectData, hcr_ids
315	) -> set[int]:
316	    """Keep only cells whose approximate centroid lies in the HCR center
317	    ¼ FOV (linear half-width), using the cheap pickle-tile-bbox center
318	    so we avoid paying zarr I/O for cells we'd drop anyway."""
319	    with open(_hcr_metrics_path(sid), "rb") as f:
320	        metrics = pickle.load(f)
321	    ids = np.array(sorted(int(i) for i in hcr_ids if int(i) in metrics))
322	    if ids.size == 0:
323	        return set()
324	    bb = np.stack([np.asarray(metrics[int(i)]["global_bbox"], dtype=float)
325	                   for i in ids])
326	    zc = 0.5 * (bb[:, 0] + bb[:, 3])
327	    yc = 0.5 * (bb[:, 1] + bb[:, 4])
328	    xc = 0.5 * (bb[:, 2] + bb[:, 5])
329	    zyx_px = np.column_stack([zc, yc, xc]).copy()
330	    zyx_px[:, 1] /= HCR_SEG_XY_DOWNSAMPLE
331	    zyx_px[:, 2] /= HCR_SEG_XY_DOWNSAMPLE
332	    xyz_um = hcr_px_to_um(zyx_px, s)[:, [2, 1, 0]]
333	    x_um, y_um = xyz_um[:, 0], xyz_um[:, 1]
334	    cx, cy = 0.5 * (x_um.min() + x_um.max()), 0.5 * (y_um.min() + y_um.max())
335	    qx = (x_um.max() - x_um.min()) / 4
336	    qy = (y_um.max() - y_um.min()) / 4
337	    mask = ((x_um >= cx - qx) & (x_um <= cx + qx)
338	            & (y_um >= cy - qy) & (y_um <= cy + qy))
339	    return set(int(i) for i in ids[mask])
340	
341	
342	def estimate_sxy_roi_area(
343	    sid: str,
344	    strict_hcr_ids=None,
345	    center_fov_quarter: bool = True,
346	) -> dict:
347	    """Estimate sxy from CZ↔HCR per-cell tight xy-bbox area ratio.
348	
349	    Parameters
350	    ----------
351	    sid : str
352	        Spot-subject id (others raise `ValueError`).
353	    strict_hcr_ids : iterable of int, optional
354	        HCR cell ids to use. Defaults to the BIC-GMM strict-GFP+ set
355	        from ``07b_gfp_intersection_threshold.strict_gfp_df``.
356	    center_fov_quarter : bool
357	        If True (default), restrict HCR cells to the center ¼ FOV so
358	        the CZ and HCR footprints match in aspect.
359	
360	    Returns
361	    -------
362	    dict
363	        Includes ``sxy_median``, ``sxy_mean``, ``sxy_gt``,
364	        per-side medians / means, cell counts, depth spans, and the
365	        strict-cutoff metadata.
366	    """
367	    if sid not in SPOT_SUBJECTS:
368	        raise ValueError(
369	            f"{sid}: intensity/unsupported subject — no HCR "
370	            "cell_body_segmentation/metrics.pickle"
371	        )
372	    s = load_subject(sid)
373	    info = analyze_subject(s)
374	    cz_surf, hcr_surf = info["cz_surface"], info["hcr_surface"]
375	
376	    # default strict-GFP+ set via the BIC-GMM intersection module
377	    strict_cutoff = None
378	    n_components = None
379	    if strict_hcr_ids is None:
380	        import importlib.util
381	        spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(
382	            "_gfp_thr", THIS / "07b_gfp_intersection_threshold.py"
383	        )
384	        _gfp_thr = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
385	        sys.modules["_gfp_thr"] = _gfp_thr
386	        spec.loader.exec_module(_gfp_thr)  # type: ignore
387	        gi = _gfp_thr.analyze_subject(sid)
388	        strict_cutoff = float(gi.cutoff_linear)
389	        n_components = int(gi.n_components)
390	        strict_df = _gfp_thr.strict_gfp_df(sid, strict_cutoff)
391	        strict_hcr_ids = set(int(x) for x in strict_df["hcr_id"].values)
392	    else:
393	        strict_hcr_ids = set(int(x) for x in strict_hcr_ids)
394	
395	    if center_fov_quarter:
396	        fov_ids = _prefilter_center_fov(sid, s, strict_hcr_ids)
397	    else:
398	        fov_ids = strict_hcr_ids
399	
400	    cz_df_all = cz_cell_tight_bboxes(sid, s, cz_surf)
401	    hcr_df_all = hcr_cell_tight_bboxes(sid, s, hcr_surf, fov_ids)
402	
403	    cz_span = float(np.nanpercentile(cz_df_all.depth_um, 99))
404	    hcr_span = float(np.nanpercentile(hcr_df_all.depth_um, 99))
405	
406	    cz_mask = (cz_df_all["depth_um"] >= D_SKIN_UM) & (cz_df_all["depth_um"] <= cz_span)
407	    hcr_mask = (hcr_df_all["depth_um"] >= D_SKIN_UM) & (hcr_df_all["depth_um"] <= hcr_span)
408	    cz_df = cz_df_all[cz_mask].copy()
409	    hcr_df = hcr_df_all[hcr_mask].copy()
410	
411	    med_cz = float(cz_df.xy_area_um2.median())
412	    med_hcr = float(hcr_df.xy_area_um2.median())
413	    mean_cz = float(cz_df.xy_area_um2.mean())
414	    mean_hcr = float(hcr_df.xy_area_um2.mean())
415	    sxy_med = float(np.sqrt(med_hcr / med_cz))
416	    sxy_mean = float(np.sqrt(mean_hcr / mean_cz))
417	
418	    fit = fit_anisotropic_similarity(*landmark_pairs_um(s, active_only=True))
419	    sxy_gt = float(np.sqrt(fit.scales[0] * fit.scales[1]))
420	
421	    return {
422	        "sid": sid,
423	        "sxy_median": sxy_med,
424	        "sxy_mean": sxy_mean,
425	        "sxy_gt": sxy_gt,
426	        "err_pct_median": 100 * (sxy_med - sxy_gt) / sxy_gt,
427	        "err_pct_mean": 100 * (sxy_mean - sxy_gt) / sxy_gt,
428	        "n_cz": int(len(cz_df)),
429	        "n_hcr_strict": int(len(hcr_df)),
430	        "cz_area_median": med_cz,
431	        "hcr_area_median": med_hcr,
432	        "cz_area_mean": mean_cz,
433	        "hcr_area_mean": mean_hcr,
434	        "cz_span_um": cz_span,
435	        "hcr_span_um": hcr_span,
436	        "center_fov_quarter": bool(center_fov_quarter),
437	        "strict_cutoff_linear": strict_cutoff,
438	        "n_components_bic": n_components,
439	        "n_hcr_input": len(strict_hcr_ids),
440	        "n_hcr_center_fov": len(fov_ids),
441	    }
442	
443	
444	def _cz_xyz_um(s: SubjectData) -> np.ndarray:
445	    arr = s.cz_centroids[["z_px", "y_px", "x_px"]].to_numpy(float)
446	    return cz_px_to_um(arr, s)[:, [2, 1, 0]]
447	
448	
449	def _hcr_gfp_xyz_um(s: SubjectData, strict_hcr_ids) -> np.ndarray:
450	    """Return HCR strict-GFP+ centroids as (x, y, z) µm via hcr_id join."""
451	    want = set(int(x) for x in strict_hcr_ids)
452	    if not want or s.hcr_centroids.empty:
453	        return np.zeros((0, 3))
454	    px = s.hcr_centroids.copy()
455	    keep = px["hcr_id"].astype(int).isin(want)
456	    arr = px.loc[keep, ["z_px", "y_px", "x_px"]].to_numpy(float)
457	    if arr.size == 0:
458	        return np.zeros((0, 3))
459	    return hcr_px_to_um(arr, s)[:, [2, 1, 0]]
460	
461	
462	def estimate_sz_roi_density(
463	    sid: str,
464	    sxy: float | None = None,
465	    strict_hcr_ids=None,
466	) -> dict:
467	    """Estimate sz by ROI-density conservation across matched CZ ↔ HCR boxes.
468	
469	    Under isotropic xy stretch ``sxy`` and axial stretch ``sz``, the same
470	    physical tissue occupies a volume that is ``sxy² · sz`` larger in
471	    HCR than in CZ. With the same cell count N in both, the native
472	    densities satisfy ``ρ_CZ / ρ_HCR = sxy² · sz``.
473	
474	    Recipe
475	    ------
476	    1. ``sxy`` from :func:`estimate_sxy_roi_area` (tight-bbox area ratio)
477	       unless supplied.
478	    2. R1 (``coarse_align_revised``) projects CZ centroid mean into the
479	       HCR µm frame — the HCR box centre. The box xy extent is
480	       ``sxy × (CZ xy extent)`` so that after undoing the stretch the
481	       box represents the same physical column as the CZ FOV.
482	    3. In CZ native: keep centroids with pia depth ∈ [d_skin, p99];
483	       ``V_CZ = A_CZ · (d_cz_span − d_skin)``, ``N_CZ`` = surviving count.
484	    4. In HCR native: strict-GFP+ centroids whose xy lies in the R1-
485	       centred matched box and whose pia depth ∈ [d_skin, p99];
486	       ``V_HCR = (sxy² · A_CZ) · (d_hcr_span − d_skin)``.
487	    5. ``sz = (ρ_CZ / ρ_HCR) / sxy² = (N_CZ · T_HCR) / (N_HCR · T_CZ)``.
488	
489	    Notes
490	    -----
491	    - ``A_CZ`` is the xy AABB of CZ centroids that survive the cortex
492	      filter (not the full FOV, so skin/outside-FOV volume isn't
493	      over-counted).
494	    - Accuracy depends on (i) sxy (which propagates as 1/sxy²), and
495	      (ii) strict-GFP+ vs CZ-active cell-count matching — 07c showed
496	      integrated GFP+/truth ≈ 1.0, so bulk density should be unbiased.
497	    """
498	    if sid not in SPOT_SUBJECTS:
499	        raise ValueError(
500	            f"{sid}: intensity/unsupported subject — no HCR "
501	            "cell_body_segmentation/metrics.pickle"
502	        )
503	    s = load_subject(sid)
504	    info = analyze_subject(s)
505	    cz_surf, hcr_surf = info["cz_surface"], info["hcr_surface"]
506	
507	    n_components = None
508	    strict_cutoff = None
509	    if sxy is None:
510	        roi = estimate_sxy_roi_area(sid)
511	        sxy = float(roi["sxy_median"])
512	        n_components = roi.get("n_components_bic")
513	        strict_cutoff = roi.get("strict_cutoff_linear")
514	
515	    if strict_hcr_ids is None:
516	        import importlib.util
517	        spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(
518	            "_gfp_thr", THIS / "07b_gfp_intersection_threshold.py"
519	        )
520	        _gfp_thr = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
521	        sys.modules["_gfp_thr"] = _gfp_thr
522	        spec.loader.exec_module(_gfp_thr)  # type: ignore
523	        gi = _gfp_thr.analyze_subject(sid)
524	        strict_cutoff = float(gi.cutoff_linear)
525	        n_components = int(gi.n_components)
526	        strict_df = _gfp_thr.strict_gfp_df(sid, strict_cutoff)
527	        strict_hcr_ids = set(int(x) for x in strict_df["hcr_id"].values)
528	
529	    cz_um = _cz_xyz_um(s)
530	    gfp_um = _hcr_gfp_xyz_um(s, strict_hcr_ids)
531	    if len(gfp_um) == 0:
532	        raise RuntimeError(f"{sid}: no strict GFP+ centroids")
533	
534	    # CZ side — cortex band + AABB
535	    cz_depth = depth_from_surface(cz_um, cz_surf)
536	    cz_span = float(np.nanpercentile(cz_depth, 99))
537	    cz_mask = (cz_depth >= D_SKIN_UM) & (cz_depth <= cz_span)
538	    n_cz = int(cz_mask.sum())
539	    if n_cz == 0:
540	        raise RuntimeError(f"{sid}: no CZ cortex cells")
541	    cz_xy = cz_um[cz_mask, :2]
542	    cz_x_span = float(cz_xy[:, 0].max() - cz_xy[:, 0].min())
543	    cz_y_span = float(cz_xy[:, 1].max() - cz_xy[:, 1].min())
544	    cz_A = cz_x_span * cz_y_span
545	    cz_T = cz_span - D_SKIN_UM
546	    cz_V = cz_A * cz_T
547	
548	    # R1 to locate CZ centroid mean in HCR frame (rigid only; ignore
549	    # R1's own scales — sxy is trusted from the area estimator).
550	    fit = coarse_align_revised(
551	        cz_um, gfp_um, cz_surf, hcr_surf, aniso_refine=False
552	    )
553	    hcr_box_center = fit.minimal_translation  # (x, y, z) µm in HCR
554	
555	    # HCR matched box — xy at the R1-projected CZ centre, half-widths
556	    # = sxy × (CZ half-spans), so A_HCR = sxy² · A_CZ
557	    half_x = 0.5 * sxy * cz_x_span
558	    half_y = 0.5 * sxy * cz_y_span
559	    box_x0 = hcr_box_center[0] - half_x
560	    box_x1 = hcr_box_center[0] + half_x
561	    box_y0 = hcr_box_center[1] - half_y
562	    box_y1 = hcr_box_center[1] + half_y
563	    hcr_A = (box_x1 - box_x0) * (box_y1 - box_y0)  # = sxy² · cz_A
564	
565	    # HCR side — depth band on GFP+ cells inside the matched xy box
566	    gfp_depth = depth_from_surface(gfp_um, hcr_surf)
567	    in_box_xy = (
568	        (gfp_um[:, 0] >= box_x0) & (gfp_um[:, 0] <= box_x1)
569	        & (gfp_um[:, 1] >= box_y0) & (gfp_um[:, 1] <= box_y1)
570	    )
571	    gfp_in_box_depth = gfp_depth[in_box_xy]
572	    if gfp_in_box_depth.size == 0:
573	        raise RuntimeError(f"{sid}: no HCR strict-GFP+ cells in matched box")
574	    hcr_span = float(np.nanpercentile(gfp_in_box_depth, 99))
575	    hcr_mask = in_box_xy & (gfp_depth >= D_SKIN_UM) & (gfp_depth <= hcr_span)
576	    n_hcr = int(hcr_mask.sum())
577	    hcr_T = hcr_span - D_SKIN_UM
578	    hcr_V = hcr_A * hcr_T
579	
580	    rho_cz = n_cz / cz_V
581	    rho_hcr = n_hcr / hcr_V if hcr_V > 0 else float("nan")
582	    if rho_hcr == 0 or not np.isfinite(rho_hcr):
583	        raise RuntimeError(f"{sid}: degenerate HCR density")
584	    sz_est = (rho_cz / rho_hcr) / (sxy ** 2)
585	
586	    fit_gt = fit_anisotropic_similarity(*landmark_pairs_um(s, active_only=True))
587	    sxy_gt = float(np.sqrt(fit_gt.scales[0] * fit_gt.scales[1]))
588	    sz_gt = float(fit_gt.scales[2])
589	
590	    return {
591	        "sid": sid,
592	        "sz": sz_est,
593	        "sz_gt": sz_gt,
594	        "err_pct": 100 * (sz_est - sz_gt) / sz_gt,
595	        "sxy_used": float(sxy),
596	        "sxy_gt": sxy_gt,
597	        "n_cz": n_cz,
598	        "n_hcr": n_hcr,
599	        "cz_T_um": cz_T,
600	        "hcr_T_um": hcr_T,
601	        "cz_A_um2": cz_A,
602	        "hcr_A_um2": hcr_A,
603	        "rho_cz_per_um3": rho_cz,
604	        "rho_hcr_per_um3": rho_hcr,
605	        "hcr_box_xy": [box_x0, box_x1, box_y0, box_y1],
606	        "r1_translation": hcr_box_center.tolist(),
607	        "strict_cutoff_linear": strict_cutoff,
608	        "n_components_bic": n_components,
609	    }
610	
611	
612	if __name__ == "__main__":
613	    import json
614	    cmd = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "both"
615	    if cmd == "sxy":
616	        sids, fn = sys.argv[2:] or sorted(SPOT_SUBJECTS), estimate_sxy_roi_area
617	    elif cmd == "sz":
618	        sids, fn = sys.argv[2:] or sorted(SPOT_SUBJECTS), estimate_sz_roi_density
619	    else:
620	        sids = sys.argv[1:] or sorted(SPOT_SUBJECTS)
621	        fn = None
622	
623	    if fn is None:
624	        print(f"{'sid':<8} {'sxy':>6} {'sxy_GT':>7} {'sxy_err':>8}  "
625	              f"{'sz':>6} {'sz_GT':>7} {'sz_err':>8}")
626	        for sid in sids:
627	            try:
628	                rsxy = estimate_sxy_roi_area(sid)
629	                rsz = estimate_sz_roi_density(sid, sxy=rsxy["sxy_median"])
630	            except Exception as e:
631	                print(f"{sid}: ERROR {e}")
632	                continue
633	            print(f"{sid:<8} {rsxy['sxy_median']:>6.3f} "
634	                  f"{rsxy['sxy_gt']:>7.3f} {rsxy['err_pct_median']:>+7.1f}%  "
635	                  f"{rsz['sz']:>6.3f} {rsz['sz_gt']:>7.3f} "
636	                  f"{rsz['err_pct']:>+7.1f}%")
637	    else:
638	        out = [fn(sid) for sid in sids]
639	        print(json.dumps(out, indent=2, default=float))
640

content.text: Now let me check the benchmark_analysis.py for load_hcr_volume and the tight-bbox cache README:

content.content:
1	# cached_hcr_cell_tight_bbox
2	
3	Per-cell **tight** bounding boxes for HCR cell-body-segmentation labels.
4	
5	## Why this cache exists
6	
7	`cell_body_segmentation/metrics.pickle` stores a per-cell
8	`global_bbox`, but it is the bbox of the segmentation **tile/chunk**
9	that contains the cell — not the tight bbox of the cell itself.
10	Median occupancy (cell voxels / bbox voxels) is ~0.016 for
11	788406. Cross-section / diameter estimates computed from the
12	pickle bbox are therefore wrong by 10× or more.
13	
14	The tight bbox has to be recovered from the raw
15	`segmentation_mask.zarr` volume by reading a crop and locating
16	the label voxels. This is the canonical place to store those
17	tight-bbox measurements so downstream sessions don't repeat
18	the I/O.
19	
20	## Files
21	
22	```
23	{sid}_hcr_cell_tight_bbox_v1.parquet   per-subject tight-bbox table
24	README.md                              this file
25	```
26	
27	Subjects present depend on which ones have been populated
28	(any session can call `07e_sxy_from_roi_area.compute_tight_hcr_bboxes`
29	to extend the cache).
30	
31	## Schema (v1)
32	
33	| column       | dtype | units         | description                                  |
34	| ------------ | ----- | ------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
35	| `hcr_id`     | int64 | —             | HCR cellpose label id                         |
36	| `zmin_vox`   | int64 | zarr voxels   | tight bbox: first z-slice (inclusive)         |
37	| `zmax_vox`   | int64 | zarr voxels   | tight bbox: last z-slice + 1 (exclusive)      |
38	| `ymin_vox`   | int64 | zarr voxels   | tight bbox y-start (inclusive)                |
39	| `ymax_vox`   | int64 | zarr voxels   | tight bbox y-end (exclusive)                  |
40	| `xmin_vox`   | int64 | zarr voxels   | tight bbox x-start (inclusive)                |
41	| `xmax_vox`   | int64 | zarr voxels   | tight bbox x-end (exclusive)                  |
42	| `volume_vox` | int64 | voxels        | actual voxel count (matches pickle `volume`)  |
43	| `zc_vox`     | float | zarr voxels   | voxel-weighted centroid (z)                   |
44	| `yc_vox`     | float | zarr voxels   | voxel-weighted centroid (y)                   |
45	| `xc_vox`     | float | zarr voxels   | voxel-weighted centroid (x)                   |
46	
47	Slice convention is NumPy-style (`[lo:hi]` half-open).
48	HCR zarr voxel size varies by subject (see
49	`benchmark_data_loader.load_subject(sid)` →
50	`s.hcr_xy_um`, `s.hcr_z_um`). As of 2026-04, all spot subjects
51	use xy=0.9865 µm, z=1.0 µm.
52	
53	## Source
54	
55	Generated from
56	`/root/capsule/data/HCR_{sid}_*/cell_body_segmentation/segmentation_mask.zarr`
57	(main array `"0"`, shape `(1, 1, Z, Y, X)`, uint32 / uint16).
58	For each hcr_id we read a crop covering the pickle tile bbox,
59	mask by `== hcr_id`, and record the tight (zmin..xmax) and
60	centroid of the masked voxels.
61	
62	## Producer
63	
64	`dev_code/07e_sxy_from_roi_area.py :: compute_tight_hcr_bboxes`.
65	Subsequent sessions may import it; the cache is additive
66	(new hcr_ids are merged into the existing parquet).
67	
68	## Subjects covered
69	
70	Currently only the four spot subjects
71	(788406, 790322, 767018, 782149) have HCR
72	`cell_body_segmentation/metrics.pickle`. 755252 and 767022
73	(intensity subjects) don't have the pickle so this cache
74	doesn't apply to them yet.
75

1066	def load_hcr_volume(
1067	    s: SubjectData,
1068	    channel: str = "405",
1069	    level: int = 4,
1070	) -> tuple[np.ndarray, float, float]:
1071	    """Load a full HCR pyramid level as (Z, Y, X) into memory.
1072	
1073	    Level 4 is a good default for surface estimation: typical shape ~
1074	    (250, 580, 580) at ~4 um XY / 4 um Z — fits in ~130 MB.
1075	    Returns (volume, xy_um, z_um).
1076	    """
1077	    import zarr
1078	
1079	    zarr_path = s.hcr_dir / "image_tile_fusing" / "fused" / f"channel_{channel}.zarr"
1080	    if not zarr_path.exists():
1081	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"No HCR fused zarr at {zarr_path}")
1082	    z = zarr.open(str(zarr_path), mode="r")
1083	    vol = np.asarray(z[str(level)][0, 0])
1084	    xy_um, z_um = hcr_level_resolution(s, level)
1085	    return vol, xy_um, z_um
1086	
1087	
1088	def load_hcr_combined(
1089	    s: SubjectData,
1090	    channels: list[str] | None = None,
1091	    level: int = 4,
1092	) -> tuple[np.ndarray, float, float, list[str]]:
1093	    """Load and combine multiple HCR channels into a normalized tissue-signal
1094	    volume.  Each channel is background-subtracted and scaled by its 99.9th
1095	    percentile before summation.  This amplifies the actual tissue boundary
1096	    over autofluorescence variability and zero-padded edges.
1097	
1098	    Returns (combined, xy_um, z_um, channels_used).
1099	    """
1100	    if channels is None:
1101	        channels = list_hcr_channels(s)
1102	    if not channels:
1103	        raise FileNotFoundError("No HCR channels for combined load")
1104	
1105	    combined: np.ndarray | None = None
1106	    xy_um = z_um = None
1107	    used: list[str] = []
1108	    for ch in channels:
1109	        try:
1110	            vol, xy_um, z_um = load_hcr_volume(s, channel=ch, level=level)
1111	        except FileNotFoundError:
1112	            continue
1113	        vol = vol.astype(np.float32, copy=False)
1114	        vmin = float(np.percentile(vol, 50))
1115	        vmax = float(np.percentile(vol, 99.9))
1116	        span = max(vmax - vmin, 1.0)
1117	        norm = np.clip((vol - vmin) / span, 0.0, None)
1118	        combined = norm if combined is None else combined + norm
1119	        used.append(ch)
1120	    if combined is None:
1121	        raise FileNotFoundError("Could not load any HCR channel")
1122	    return combined, float(xy_um), float(z_um), used
1123	
1124	
1125	def estimate_pia_surface_image_autoselect(
1126	    s: SubjectData,
1127	    *,
1128	    level: int = 4,
1129	    qcon_n_threshold: float = 0.9,
1130	    v21_module=None,
1131	):
1132	    """Image-only pia surface with candidate-bank auto-selection.
1133	
1134	    Fits the session-03 v2.1 image estimator on a small bank of
1135	    (channel-subset × target_quantile) candidates, scores each in the
1136	    reference combined HCR volume using
1137	    :func:`score_surface_quality`, and returns the highest-Q candidate
1138	    whose normalised contrast ``Qcon_n >= qcon_n_threshold``.

content.text: Now I understand the full picture. The `hcr_gfp_df` for spot subjects has `density` (and sometimes `volume`, `counts`), but does NOT have columns named `peakgauss3_density_p0.1` etc. — those are the **threshold method names** used to filter, not columns in the output df. The GFP+ features in the task spec refer to the density/mean_minus_bg values. Let me check what "GFP+ score" means in context:

"""Per-ROI feature extractor for the HCR ROI pass/fail classifier (S11).

Public API
----------
extract_roi_features(s, level=2, cache=True) -> pd.DataFrame
    One row per HCR ROI for subject s.  Cached to
    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/<sid>_features.parquet.

Coordinate conventions
----------------------
- seg mask = segmentation_mask.zarr["0"]: level-0 voxels,
  shape (1,1,Z,Y,X).  xy voxel size = s.hcr_seg_xy_um,
  z voxel size = s.hcr_z_um.
- metrics.pickle global_bbox: level-0 voxel indices (same frame).
- hcr_centroids: level-2 pixel frame, xy = s.hcr_xy_um, z = s.hcr_z_um.
- fused channel zarrs at level-0 are in the same spatial frame as the
  seg mask (both are 0.247 µm/vox xy for spot subjects, 0.247 µm/vox
  for intensity subjects too — the seg input was the level-0 fused stack).
- fused channel level-2 is 4× coarser in xy (= s.hcr_xy_um).

For intensity stats we read per-ROI level-0 crops from fused channel
zarrs — this is exact and avoids loading the full volume.

For neighbour density we use hcr_centroids (level-2) converted to µm.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import gc
import glob
import json
import os
import pickle
import sys
import time
import warnings
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import scipy.ndimage as ndi
import zarr
from scipy.spatial import cKDTree
from tqdm import tqdm

# ── suppress the zarr empty-filters warning for cleaner logs ──────────────────
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=UserWarning, module="zarr")

THIS = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
if str(THIS) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(THIS))

from benchmark_data_loader import (
    DATA_DIR,
    HCR_SEG_XY_DOWNSAMPLE,
    SubjectData,
    hcr_px_to_um,
)

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# paths
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ROI_QUALITY_CACHE = Path("/root/capsule/code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality")
ROI_QUALITY_CACHE.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

TIGHT_BBOX_CACHE = Path("/root/capsule/code/dev_code/cached_hcr_cell_tight_bbox")
TIGHT_BBOX_CACHE.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# Probe channel per subject.  Spot subjects have 561; intensity subjects have 514.
PROBE_CHANNEL: Dict[str, str] = {
    "755252": "514",
    "767022": "514",
}
PROBE_CHANNEL_DEFAULT = "561"

SPOT_SUBJECTS = frozenset({"788406", "790322", "767018", "782149"})
INTENSITY_SUBJECTS = frozenset({"755252", "767022"})

# k for kNN
KNN_KS = [1, 3, 5]
KNN_RADIUS_UM = 30.0

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# path helpers
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _seg_zarr_path(s: SubjectData) -> Path:
    p = s.hcr_dir / "cell_body_segmentation" / "segmentation_mask.zarr"
    if not p.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"seg mask not found: {p}")
    return p


def _fused_zarr_path(s: SubjectData, channel: str) -> Optional[Path]:
    p = s.hcr_dir / "image_tile_fusing" / "fused" / f"channel_{channel}.zarr"
    return p if p.exists() else None


def _metrics_path(s: SubjectData) -> Optional[Path]:
    p = s.hcr_dir / "cell_body_segmentation" / "metrics.pickle"
    return p if p.exists() else None


def _tight_bbox_cache_path(sid: str) -> Path:
    return TIGHT_BBOX_CACHE / f"{sid}_hcr_cell_tight_bbox_v1.parquet"


def _features_cache_path(sid: str) -> Path:
    return ROI_QUALITY_CACHE / f"{sid}_features.parquet"


def _meta_path(sid: str) -> Path:
    return ROI_QUALITY_CACHE / f"{sid}_meta.json"


# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# tight-bbox helpers (extend cache for intensity subjects via full zarr scan)
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _load_tight_bboxes_from_cache(sid: str) -> Optional[pd.DataFrame]:
    p = _tight_bbox_cache_path(sid)
    if p.exists():
        return pd.read_parquet(p)
    return None


def _scan_seg_zarr_for_bboxes(
    seg_arr,
    chunk_z: int = 128,
    verbose: bool = True,
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Scan the full seg zarr volume in z-chunks and accumulate per-label tight bboxes.

    Used for intensity subjects (755252, 767022) that have no metrics.pickle.
    Accumulates min/max per axis and voxel count per label without storing
    the full volume.

    seg_arr : zarr array with shape (1,1,Z,Y,X) uint32.
    Returns DataFrame with schema matching cached_hcr_cell_tight_bbox v1.
    """
    _, _, Z, Y, X = seg_arr.shape
    # dicts: label -> running statistics
    zmin: Dict[int, int] = {}
    zmax: Dict[int, int] = {}
    ymin: Dict[int, int] = {}
    ymax: Dict[int, int] = {}
    xmin: Dict[int, int] = {}
    xmax: Dict[int, int] = {}
    vox_count: Dict[int, int] = {}
    # for weighted centroid: sum of indices
    zsum: Dict[int, float] = {}
    ysum: Dict[int, float] = {}
    xsum: Dict[int, float] = {}

    z_starts = range(0, Z, chunk_z)
    for z0 in tqdm(z_starts, desc="scanning seg zarr", disable=not verbose):
        z1 = min(z0 + chunk_z, Z)
        chunk = np.asarray(seg_arr[0, 0, z0:z1, :, :])  # (dz, Y, X)
        labels_in_chunk = np.unique(chunk)
        labels_in_chunk = labels_in_chunk[labels_in_chunk != 0]
        for lbl in labels_in_chunk:
            mask = (chunk == lbl)
            zs, ys, xs = np.where(mask)
            lbl = int(lbl)
            n = int(mask.sum())
            z_global = zs + z0
            zmin[lbl] = int(z_global.min()) if lbl not in zmin else min(zmin[lbl], int(z_global.min()))
            zmax[lbl] = int(z_global.max()) if lbl not in zmax else max(zmax[lbl], int(z_global.max()))
            ymin[lbl] = int(ys.min()) if lbl not in ymin else min(ymin[lbl], int(ys.min()))
            ymax[lbl] = int(ys.max()) if lbl not in ymax else max(ymax[lbl], int(ys.max()))
            xmin[lbl] = int(xs.min()) if lbl not in xmin else min(xmin[lbl], int(xs.min()))
            xmax[lbl] = int(xs.max()) if lbl not in xmax else max(xmax[lbl], int(xs.max()))
            vox_count[lbl] = vox_count.get(lbl, 0) + n
            zsum[lbl] = zsum.get(lbl, 0.0) + float(z_global.sum())
            ysum[lbl] = ysum.get(lbl, 0.0) + float(ys.sum())
            xsum[lbl] = xsum.get(lbl, 0.0) + float(xs.sum())

    if not vox_count:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=[
            "hcr_id", "zmin_vox", "ymin_vox", "xmin_vox",
            "zmax_vox", "ymax_vox", "xmax_vox",
            "volume_vox", "zc_vox", "yc_vox", "xc_vox",
        ])

    rows = []
    for lbl in sorted(vox_count.keys()):
        n = vox_count[lbl]
        rows.append({
            "hcr_id": int(lbl),
            "zmin_vox": int(zmin[lbl]),
            # +1 to make exclusive (matching roi_area_sxy convention)
            "zmax_vox": int(zmax[lbl]) + 1,
            "ymin_vox": int(ymin[lbl]),
            "ymax_vox": int(ymax[lbl]) + 1,
            "xmin_vox": int(xmin[lbl]),
            "xmax_vox": int(xmax[lbl]) + 1,
            "volume_vox": int(n),
            "zc_vox": float(zsum[lbl] / n),
            "yc_vox": float(ysum[lbl] / n),
            "xc_vox": float(xsum[lbl] / n),
        })
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


def get_tight_bboxes(s: SubjectData, force: bool = False) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Return tight-bbox DataFrame for all labels in subject s.

    For spot subjects: uses the existing cache (extended by roi_area_sxy.py).
    For intensity subjects (no pickle): scans the full zarr once and caches.
    """
    sid = s.subject_id
    cache_path = _tight_bbox_cache_path(sid)

    if cache_path.exists() and not force:
        df = pd.read_parquet(cache_path)
        n_labels = len(s.hcr_centroids)
        print(f"  [{sid}] tight-bbox cache hit: {len(df)} entries "
              f"(vs {n_labels} centroids)")
        return df

    if sid in SPOT_SUBJECTS:
        # Spot subjects: must have pickle — use the roi_area_sxy pattern.
        # Import lazily to avoid circular deps.
        from roi_area_sxy import compute_tight_hcr_bboxes
        all_ids = s.hcr_centroids["hcr_id"].astype(int).tolist()
        return compute_tight_hcr_bboxes(sid, all_ids, verbose=True)

    # Intensity subjects: no pickle — scan the full zarr.
    print(f"  [{sid}] no metrics.pickle — scanning full seg zarr (one-time)...")
    seg_zarr = zarr.open(str(_seg_zarr_path(s)), mode="r")["0"]
    print(f"  [{sid}] seg shape: {seg_zarr.shape}, dtype: {seg_zarr.dtype}")
    t0 = time.time()
    df = _scan_seg_zarr_for_bboxes(seg_zarr, chunk_z=128, verbose=True)
    elapsed = time.time() - t0
    print(f"  [{sid}] scan done: {len(df)} labels in {elapsed:.1f}s")
    df = df.sort_values("hcr_id").reset_index(drop=True)
    df.to_parquet(cache_path, index=False)
    print(f"  [{sid}] tight-bbox saved → {cache_path}")
    return df


# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# shape features
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _shape_features_from_bbox(
    row: pd.Series,
    seg_arr,
    seg_z_um: float,
    seg_xy_um: float,
    seg_shape_ZYX: Tuple[int, int, int],
) -> Dict:
    """Compute shape features for one ROI from its tight bbox.

    Reads a small crop of the seg zarr around the tight bbox, then runs
    skimage regionprops on it.  Returns dict of scalar features.
    """
    from skimage.measure import regionprops

    hid = int(row["hcr_id"])
    zlo = int(row["zmin_vox"])
    zhi = int(row["zmax_vox"])  # exclusive
    ylo = int(row["ymin_vox"])
    yhi = int(row["ymax_vox"])  # exclusive
    xlo = int(row["xmin_vox"])
    xhi = int(row["xmax_vox"])  # exclusive

    Z, Y, X = seg_shape_ZYX

    # boundary touching: check whether tight bbox touches the volume edge
    boundary_touching = bool(
        zlo == 0 or zhi >= Z
        or ylo == 0 or yhi >= Y
        or xlo == 0 or xhi >= X
    )

    # volume and bbox dimensions in voxels
    volume_vox = int(row["volume_vox"])
    dz = zhi - zlo  # voxel extents (inclusive count = exclusive diff)
    dy = yhi - ylo
    dx = xhi - xlo
    bbox_volume_vox = dz * dy * dx

    # volume in µm³
    vol_um3 = volume_vox * seg_z_um * seg_xy_um * seg_xy_um
    bbox_z_um = dz * seg_z_um
    bbox_y_um = dy * seg_xy_um
    bbox_x_um = dx * seg_xy_um

    bbox_occupancy = volume_vox / max(bbox_volume_vox, 1)

    # aspect ratios (larger / smaller to keep >= 1)
    aspect_zy = bbox_z_um / max(bbox_y_um, 1e-9)
    aspect_zx = bbox_z_um / max(bbox_x_um, 1e-9)
    aspect_yx = bbox_y_um / max(bbox_x_um, 1e-9)

    # equivalent diameter in µm (sphere with same volume)
    equiv_diam_um = 2.0 * (3.0 * vol_um3 / (4.0 * np.pi)) ** (1.0 / 3.0)

    # read crop for regionprops (solidity, sphericity)
    crop = np.asarray(seg_arr[0, 0, zlo:zhi, ylo:yhi, xlo:xhi])
    binary = (crop == hid).astype(np.uint8)

    solidity = float("nan")
    sphericity = float("nan")
    if binary.any():
        try:
            from skimage.measure import regionprops_table
            spacing = (seg_z_um, seg_xy_um, seg_xy_um)
            props = regionprops_table(
                binary,
                properties=["area", "solidity", "area_convex",
                            "equivalent_diameter_axis_length"],
                spacing=spacing,
            )
            # solidity = area / convex_hull_area
            if "solidity" in props and len(props["solidity"]) > 0:
                solidity = float(props["solidity"][0])
            # sphericity = (36π V²)^(1/3) / A  where A = surface area in µm²
            # surface area via marching-cubes
            try:
                from skimage.measure import marching_cubes
                verts, faces, _, _ = marching_cubes(
                    binary.astype(float), level=0.5, spacing=spacing,
                    allow_degenerate=False,
                )
                from skimage.measure import mesh_surface_area
                surf_area = float(mesh_surface_area(verts, faces))
                if surf_area > 0 and vol_um3 > 0:
                    sphericity = float(
                        (36.0 * np.pi * vol_um3 ** 2) ** (1.0 / 3.0) / surf_area
                    )
            except Exception:
                pass
        except Exception:
            pass

    return {
        "volume_vox": volume_vox,
        "volume_um3": vol_um3,
        "bbox_z_extent": bbox_z_um,
        "bbox_y_extent": bbox_y_um,
        "bbox_x_extent": bbox_x_um,
        "equivalent_diameter_um": equiv_diam_um,
        "aspect_zy": aspect_zy,
        "aspect_zx": aspect_zx,
        "aspect_yx": aspect_yx,
        "solidity": solidity,
        "sphericity": sphericity,
        "boundary_touching": boundary_touching,
        "bbox_occupancy": bbox_occupancy,
    }


# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# intensity features
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _open_fused_level0(s: SubjectData, channel: str):
    """Open fused channel zarr at level-0.  Returns zarr array or None."""
    p = _fused_zarr_path(s, channel)
    if p is None:
        return None
    try:
        z = zarr.open(str(p), mode="r")
        if "0" not in z:
            return None
        return z["0"]  # shape (1,1,Z,Y,X)
    except Exception:
        return None


def _intensity_stats_for_roi(
    img_crop: np.ndarray,
    roi_mask: np.ndarray,
) -> Dict:
    """Compute intensity stats from a pre-loaded crop and binary mask.

    img_crop, roi_mask: both (dz, dy, dx), mask is boolean.
    Returns dict with mean, std, p10, p50, p90, fg_minus_margin_p50.
    """
    pxs = img_crop[roi_mask].astype(np.float32)
    if pxs.size == 0:
        nan6 = {k: float("nan") for k in
                ["mean", "std", "p10", "p50", "p90", "fg_minus_margin_p50"]}
        return nan6

    mean_ = float(np.mean(pxs))
    std_ = float(np.std(pxs))
    p10, p50, p90 = float(np.percentile(pxs, 10)), float(np.percentile(pxs, 50)), float(np.percentile(pxs, 90))

    # 1-voxel dilated shell minus the ROI itself
    dilated = ndi.binary_dilation(roi_mask, iterations=1)
    shell_mask = dilated & ~roi_mask
    shell_pxs = img_crop[shell_mask].astype(np.float32)
    shell_p50 = float(np.median(shell_pxs)) if shell_pxs.size > 0 else float("nan")
    fg_minus_margin = p50 - shell_p50

    return {
        "mean": mean_,
        "std": std_,
        "p10": p10,
        "p50": p50,
        "p90": p90,
        "fg_minus_margin_p50": fg_minus_margin,
    }


def _c405_nuclear_features(
    img405_crop: np.ndarray,
    roi_mask: np.ndarray,
) -> Dict:
    """405 nuclear agreement features from a level-0 crop.

    img405_crop: (dz,dy,dx) float32 or uint.
    roi_mask: (dz,dy,dx) bool.
    """
    img = img405_crop.astype(np.float32)

    # eroded core: 1-voxel erosion of ROI mask
    core_mask = ndi.binary_erosion(roi_mask, iterations=1)
    core_pxs = img[core_mask].astype(np.float32)
    c405_core_p90 = float(np.percentile(core_pxs, 90)) if core_pxs.size > 0 else float("nan")

    # dilated shell: 1-voxel dilation minus ROI
    dilated = ndi.binary_dilation(roi_mask, iterations=1)
    shell_mask = dilated & ~roi_mask
    shell_pxs = img[shell_mask].astype(np.float32)
    c405_shell_p90 = float(np.percentile(shell_pxs, 90)) if shell_pxs.size > 0 else float("nan")

    c405_core_minus_shell_p90 = (
        c405_core_p90 - c405_shell_p90
        if np.isfinite(c405_core_p90) and np.isfinite(c405_shell_p90)
        else float("nan")
    )

    # peak-inside: is the max-405 voxel inside the ROI mask?
    c405_peak_inside = False
    if roi_mask.any() and img.size > 0:
        max_idx = np.unravel_index(np.argmax(img), img.shape)
        c405_peak_inside = bool(roi_mask[max_idx])

    return {
        "c405_core_p90": c405_core_p90,
        "c405_shell_p90": c405_shell_p90,
        "c405_peak_inside": c405_peak_inside,
        "c405_core_minus_shell_p90": c405_core_minus_shell_p90,
    }


# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# neighbour density
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _build_knn_tree(s: SubjectData) -> Tuple[cKDTree, np.ndarray]:
    """Build a cKDTree on all HCR centroids in µm.

    hcr_centroids are level-2 voxels; multiply by (z_um, xy_um, xy_um).
    Returns (tree, pts_um) where pts_um[i] = (z_um, y_um, x_um) for centroid i.
    Note: the tree is built in z,y,x order (anisotropic distances are correct
    only when the units are µm in all three axes).
    """
    c = s.hcr_centroids
    if c.empty:
        return None, np.zeros((0, 3))
    arr = c[["z_px", "y_px", "x_px"]].to_numpy(float)
    # level-2 → µm
    pts_um = arr * np.array([s.hcr_z_um, s.hcr_xy_um, s.hcr_xy_um])
    tree = cKDTree(pts_um)
    return tree, pts_um


def _knn_features_for_centroids(
    tree: cKDTree,
    pts_um: np.ndarray,
    query_zyx_um: np.ndarray,
    ks: List[int],
    radius: float,
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Query the kNN tree for all query centroids at once.

    query_zyx_um: (N, 3) in µm, same ordering as tree.
    Returns DataFrame with knn_d{k} and n_neighbors_30um columns.
    """
    max_k = max(ks) + 1  # +1 because the point itself is in the tree
    N = len(query_zyx_um)
    if N == 0 or tree is None:
        cols = {f"knn_d{k}": np.full(N, float("nan")) for k in ks}
        cols["n_neighbors_30um"] = np.zeros(N, dtype=int)
        return pd.DataFrame(cols)

    n_tree = pts_um.shape[0]
    # Can't ask for more neighbours than exist
    actual_k = min(max_k, n_tree)
    dists, _ = tree.query(query_zyx_um, k=actual_k, workers=1)
    if actual_k == 1:
        dists = dists[:, np.newaxis]

    rows: Dict[str, np.ndarray] = {}
    for k in ks:
        col = f"knn_d{k}"
        # self is idx 0; k-th nearest neighbour is at column k (0-based)
        if k < actual_k:
            rows[col] = dists[:, k].astype(float)
        else:
            rows[col] = np.full(N, float("nan"))

    # count within radius (excluding self at dist=0)
    counts_r = tree.query_ball_point(query_zyx_um, r=radius, workers=1)
    # subtract self (each query point is in the tree)
    rows["n_neighbors_30um"] = np.array(
        [max(0, len(idxs) - 1) for idxs in counts_r], dtype=int
    )
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# GFP+ features
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _gfp_features(s: SubjectData) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Extract GFP+ feature columns from hcr_gfp_df.

    Returns DataFrame keyed by hcr_id with columns:
    gfp_feature_value, gfp_density (NaN if not a spot subject),
    gfp_counts (NaN if not available).
    """
    gdf = s.hcr_gfp_df
    if gdf is None or gdf.empty:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=["hcr_id", "gfp_feature_value",
                                      "gfp_density", "gfp_counts"])

    out = pd.DataFrame({"hcr_id": gdf["hcr_id"].astype(int)})
    feat = s.gfp_feature_name  # 'density' or 'mean_minus_bg'

    # Primary GFP feature value (whatever the method produced)
    if feat and feat in gdf.columns:
        out["gfp_feature_value"] = gdf[feat].values
    else:
        out["gfp_feature_value"] = float("nan")

    out["gfp_density"] = gdf["density"].values if "density" in gdf.columns else float("nan")
    out["gfp_counts"] = gdf["counts"].values if "counts" in gdf.columns else float("nan")
    return out


# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# pickle-bbox consistency check
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _pickle_bbox_consistency(
    bboxes_df: pd.DataFrame,
    metrics: Optional[Dict],
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Check whether the tight zarr bbox is contained within the pickle's global_bbox.

    bboxes_df: tight-bbox parquet (columns zmin_vox .. xmax_vox).
    metrics: the loaded pickle dict (hid -> {'global_bbox': array}).
    Returns DataFrame with columns hcr_id, tight_bbox_in_pickle_bbox,
    volume_pickle_minus_zarr.

    For intensity subjects (metrics=None), all True / 0.
    """
    hids = bboxes_df["hcr_id"].astype(int).values
    in_pickle = np.ones(len(hids), dtype=bool)
    vol_diff = np.zeros(len(hids), dtype=float)

    if metrics is not None:
        for i, hid in enumerate(hids):
            m = metrics.get(int(hid))
            if m is None:
                in_pickle[i] = False
                continue
            bb = np.asarray(m["global_bbox"], dtype=int)
            # pickle bbox: [zlo, ylo, xlo, zhi, yhi, xhi] (inclusive on both ends)
            pz0, py0, px0, pz1, py1, px1 = bb
            row = bboxes_df.iloc[i]
            # tight bbox is half-open [lo, hi); compare with pickle+1 (inclusive->exclusive)
            in_pickle[i] = bool(
                int(row["zmin_vox"]) >= pz0
                and int(row["zmax_vox"]) <= pz1 + 1
                and int(row["ymin_vox"]) >= py0
                and int(row["ymax_vox"]) <= py1 + 1
                and int(row["xmin_vox"]) >= px0
                and int(row["xmax_vox"]) <= px1 + 1
            )
            # volume difference: pickle volume vs tight zarr volume_vox
            pickle_vol = float(m.get("volume", float("nan")))
            zarr_vol = float(row["volume_vox"])
            vol_diff[i] = pickle_vol - zarr_vol if np.isfinite(pickle_vol) else float("nan")

    return pd.DataFrame({
        "hcr_id": hids.astype(int),
        "tight_bbox_in_pickle_bbox": in_pickle,
        "volume_pickle_minus_zarr": vol_diff,
    })


# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# main extractor
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def extract_roi_features(
    s: SubjectData,
    level: int = 2,
    cache: bool = True,
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Extract per-ROI features for all HCR ROIs in subject s.

    Parameters
    ----------
    s : SubjectData
        Loaded subject (from benchmark_data_loader.load_subject).
    level : int
        Recorded in the meta JSON.  Intensity stats are always at level-0
        regardless of this parameter (per-ROI level-0 crop approach).
    cache : bool
        If True, load from parquet if it exists; otherwise compute and write.

    Returns
    -------
    pd.DataFrame
        One row per HCR ROI, indexed by hcr_id.  See module docstring for
        column groups.
    """
    sid = s.subject_id
    cache_path = _features_cache_path(sid)
    meta_path = _meta_path(sid)

    if cache and cache_path.exists():
        print(f"  [{sid}] loading cached features from {cache_path}")
        return pd.read_parquet(cache_path)

    t_start = time.time()
    probe_ch = PROBE_CHANNEL.get(sid, PROBE_CHANNEL_DEFAULT)
    channels = ["405", "488", probe_ch, "594"]
    has_pickle = _metrics_path(s) is not None

    print(f"\n[{sid}] extracting ROI features | pickle={has_pickle} | probe={probe_ch}")

    # ── 1. tight bboxes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    print(f"  [{sid}] loading tight bboxes...")
    bboxes_df = get_tight_bboxes(s)
    print(f"  [{sid}] tight bboxes: {len(bboxes_df)} labels")
    if bboxes_df.empty:
        raise RuntimeError(f"[{sid}] no tight bboxes found")

    # align to centroid ids (truth source)
    centroid_ids = set(s.hcr_centroids["hcr_id"].astype(int).tolist())
    bboxes_df = bboxes_df[bboxes_df["hcr_id"].isin(centroid_ids)].reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f"  [{sid}] after centroid filter: {len(bboxes_df)} labels")

    # ── 2. open seg zarr ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    seg_zarr = zarr.open(str(_seg_zarr_path(s)), mode="r")["0"]
    _, _, Z_seg, Y_seg, X_seg = seg_zarr.shape
    seg_z_um = s.hcr_z_um
    seg_xy_um = s.hcr_seg_xy_um  # level-0 voxel size
    print(f"  [{sid}] seg shape: ({Z_seg},{Y_seg},{X_seg}), "
          f"seg_xy_um={seg_xy_um:.4f}, seg_z_um={seg_z_um:.4f}")

    # ── 3. open fused channel zarrs at level 0 ───────────────────────────────
    channel_zarrs = {}
    for ch in channels:
        arr = _open_fused_level0(s, ch)
        if arr is not None:
            _, _, Zc, Yc, Xc = arr.shape
            print(f"  [{sid}] channel {ch}: shape ({Zc},{Yc},{Xc})")
        else:
            print(f"  [{sid}] channel {ch}: NOT FOUND — will be NaN")
        channel_zarrs[ch] = arr

    # ── 4. load metrics.pickle if available ──────────────────────────────────
    metrics = None
    if has_pickle:
        with open(_metrics_path(s), "rb") as f:
            metrics = pickle.load(f)
        print(f"  [{sid}] metrics.pickle loaded: {len(metrics)} cells")

    # ── 5. build kNN tree ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    print(f"  [{sid}] building kNN tree...")
    knn_tree, pts_um = _build_knn_tree(s)

    # Convert tight-bbox centroids (level-0 voxels) to level-2 pixel frame,
    # then to µm, for querying the tree (which is built on hcr_centroids level-2).
    zc_l2 = bboxes_df["zc_vox"].values  # z is the same in level-0 and level-2
    yc_l2 = bboxes_df["yc_vox"].values / HCR_SEG_XY_DOWNSAMPLE
    xc_l2 = bboxes_df["xc_vox"].values / HCR_SEG_XY_DOWNSAMPLE
    query_um = np.column_stack([
        zc_l2 * s.hcr_z_um,
        yc_l2 * s.hcr_xy_um,
        xc_l2 * s.hcr_xy_um,
    ])
    print(f"  [{sid}] kNN tree size: {pts_um.shape[0]}, queries: {len(query_um)}")

    # ── 6. per-ROI loop ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    n_roi = len(bboxes_df)
    shape_rows = []
    intens_rows = {ch: [] for ch in channels}
    c405_rows = []

    for i, row in tqdm(
        bboxes_df.iterrows(),
        total=n_roi,
        desc=f"[{sid}] per-ROI",
        unit="roi",
    ):
        hid = int(row["hcr_id"])
        zlo = int(row["zmin_vox"])
        zhi = int(row["zmax_vox"])  # exclusive
        ylo = int(row["ymin_vox"])
        yhi = int(row["ymax_vox"])
        xlo = int(row["xmin_vox"])
        xhi = int(row["xmax_vox"])

        # ── shape ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        sf = _shape_features_from_bbox(
            row, seg_zarr, seg_z_um, seg_xy_um, (Z_seg, Y_seg, X_seg)
        )
        sf["hcr_id"] = hid
        shape_rows.append(sf)

        # ── intensity: read seg crop once for mask ────────────────────────────
        seg_crop = np.asarray(seg_zarr[0, 0, zlo:zhi, ylo:yhi, xlo:xhi])
        roi_mask = (seg_crop == hid)

        # ── per-channel intensity stats ───────────────────────────────────────
        img405_crop = None
        for ch in channels:
            zarr_arr = channel_zarrs[ch]
            if zarr_arr is None:
                intens_rows[ch].append({"hcr_id": hid,
                                         "mean": float("nan"), "std": float("nan"),
                                         "p10": float("nan"), "p50": float("nan"),
                                         "p90": float("nan"),
                                         "fg_minus_margin_p50": float("nan")})
                continue
            # Read level-0 crop matching seg bbox
            # (seg mask and fused level-0 are in the same spatial frame)
            img_crop = np.asarray(zarr_arr[0, 0, zlo:zhi, ylo:yhi, xlo:xhi]).astype(np.float32)
            stats = _intensity_stats_for_roi(img_crop, roi_mask)
            stats["hcr_id"] = hid
            intens_rows[ch].append(stats)
            if ch == "405":
                img405_crop = img_crop

        # ── 405 nuclear features ──────────────────────────────────────────────
        if img405_crop is not None:
            nf = _c405_nuclear_features(img405_crop, roi_mask)
        else:
            nf = {
                "c405_core_p90": float("nan"),
                "c405_shell_p90": float("nan"),
                "c405_peak_inside": False,
                "c405_core_minus_shell_p90": float("nan"),
            }
        nf["hcr_id"] = hid
        c405_rows.append(nf)

    print(f"  [{sid}] per-ROI loop done, assembling...")

    # ── 7. assemble shape df ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    shape_df = pd.DataFrame(shape_rows)

    # ── 8. intensity dfs with channel-prefixed columns ────────────────────────
    intens_dfs = []
    for ch in channels:
        df_ch = pd.DataFrame(intens_rows[ch])
        df_ch = df_ch.rename(columns={
            c: f"c{ch}_{c}" for c in df_ch.columns if c != "hcr_id"
        })
        intens_dfs.append(df_ch)

    # merge all intensity dfs
    merged_intens = intens_dfs[0]
    for df_ch in intens_dfs[1:]:
        merged_intens = merged_intens.merge(df_ch, on="hcr_id", how="outer")

    # ── 9. 405 nuclear features df ────────────────────────────────────────────
    c405_df = pd.DataFrame(c405_rows)

    # ── 10. kNN features ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    knn_df = _knn_features_for_centroids(
        knn_tree, pts_um, query_um, KNN_KS, KNN_RADIUS_UM
    )
    knn_df["hcr_id"] = bboxes_df["hcr_id"].astype(int).values

    # ── 11. GFP+ features ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    gfp_df = _gfp_features(s)

    # ── 12. pickle consistency ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    cons_df = _pickle_bbox_consistency(bboxes_df, metrics)

    # ── 13. merge all dfs on hcr_id ──────────────────────────────────────────
    result = shape_df
    for df in [merged_intens, c405_df, knn_df]:
        result = result.merge(df, on="hcr_id", how="left")
    result = result.merge(gfp_df, on="hcr_id", how="left")
    result = result.merge(cons_df, on="hcr_id", how="left")

    # ── 14. final sort and dtype clean ────────────────────────────────────────
    result = result.sort_values("hcr_id").reset_index(drop=True)
    # Ensure boolean columns are actual bool (parquet-safe)
    bool_cols = ["boundary_touching", "c405_peak_inside", "tight_bbox_in_pickle_bbox"]
    for bc in bool_cols:
        if bc in result.columns:
            result[bc] = result[bc].astype(bool)

    elapsed = time.time() - t_start
    print(f"  [{sid}] done: {len(result)} ROIs in {elapsed:.1f}s")
    print(f"  [{sid}] columns: {list(result.columns)}")

    # sanity checks
    assert "hcr_id" in result.columns
    assert len(result) > 0, f"[{sid}] empty result!"
    # Check a few value ranges
    vol_min = result["volume_vox"].min()
    vol_max = result["volume_vox"].max()
    bnd_frac = result["boundary_touching"].mean()
    print(f"  [{sid}] volume_vox range: [{vol_min}, {vol_max}]")
    print(f"  [{sid}] boundary_touching fraction: {bnd_frac:.3f}")
    peak_frac = result["c405_peak_inside"].mean()
    print(f"  [{sid}] c405_peak_inside fraction: {peak_frac:.3f}")

    # ── 15. cache ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if cache:
        result.to_parquet(cache_path, index=False)
        print(f"  [{sid}] features saved → {cache_path}")

    # ── 16. meta JSON ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    channels_available = [ch for ch in channels if channel_zarrs.get(ch) is not None]
    meta = {
        "subject_id": sid,
        "channels_used": channels_available,
        "probe_channel": probe_ch,
        "has_pickle": has_pickle,
        "total_rois": int(len(result)),
        "extraction_timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
        "level_recorded": level,
        "intensity_stats_level": 0,
        "seg_shape_ZYX": [int(Z_seg), int(Y_seg), int(X_seg)],
        "seg_xy_um": float(seg_xy_um),
        "seg_z_um": float(seg_z_um),
        "hcr_xy_um_level2": float(s.hcr_xy_um),
        "hcr_z_um": float(s.hcr_z_um),
        "elapsed_seconds": float(elapsed),
        "quirks": _subject_quirks(sid, has_pickle, channels_available),
    }
    with open(meta_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(meta, f, indent=2)
    print(f"  [{sid}] meta saved → {meta_path}")

    return result


def _subject_quirks(sid: str, has_pickle: bool, channels_available: List[str]) -> List[str]:
    quirks = []
    if not has_pickle:
        quirks.append(
            "No metrics.pickle — tight bboxes recovered via full zarr scan. "
            "pickle-bbox consistency columns are all True/0."
        )
    missing_ch = [c for c in ["405", "488"] if c not in channels_available]
    if missing_ch:
        quirks.append(f"Missing channels: {missing_ch}. Intensity stats are NaN for these.")
    if sid in INTENSITY_SUBJECTS:
        quirks.append("Intensity subject — GFP+ feature is mean_minus_bg, not spot density.")
    return quirks

content.text: Now create the driver script:

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Driver: extract per-ROI features for all 6 benchmark subjects.

Usage:
    python 01_extract_features.py [--force]

    --force  Ignore existing feature caches and recompute everything.

Outputs:
    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/<sid>_features.parquet
    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/<sid>_meta.json
    code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/outputs/feature_summary.csv
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

# ── path setup ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEV_CODE = Path("/root/capsule/code/dev_code")
SESSION_DIR = Path("/root/capsule/code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality")
OUTPUTS_DIR = SESSION_DIR / "outputs"
OUTPUTS_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

sys.path.insert(0, str(DEV_CODE))

from benchmark_data_loader import load_subject, BENCHMARK_SUBJECTS
from roi_quality import extract_roi_features, _features_cache_path

SUBJECTS = BENCHMARK_SUBJECTS  # ['755252','767018','767022','782149','788406','790322']

# Per-subject wall-clock budget before we fall back to smoke-test mode
WALLCLOCK_BUDGET_S = 30 * 60  # 30 minutes


def _summary_row(sid: str, df: pd.DataFrame, elapsed: float) -> dict:
    """Build one summary row for the CSV."""
    n = len(df)
    bnd = float(df["boundary_touching"].mean()) if "boundary_touching" in df.columns else float("nan")
    peak = float(df["c405_peak_inside"].mean()) if "c405_peak_inside" in df.columns else float("nan")
    med_vol = float(df["volume_um3"].median()) if "volume_um3" in df.columns else float("nan")
    in_pickle = float(df["tight_bbox_in_pickle_bbox"].mean()) if "tight_bbox_in_pickle_bbox" in df.columns else float("nan")
    med_sol = float(df["solidity"].median()) if "solidity" in df.columns else float("nan")
    med_sph = float(df["sphericity"].median()) if "sphericity" in df.columns else float("nan")
    med_knn1 = float(df["knn_d1"].median()) if "knn_d1" in df.columns else float("nan")
    med_c405_contrast = float(df["c405_core_minus_shell_p90"].median()) if "c405_core_minus_shell_p90" in df.columns else float("nan")
    med_gfp = float(df["gfp_feature_value"].median()) if "gfp_feature_value" in df.columns else float("nan")

    return {
        "sid": sid,
        "n_roi": n,
        "elapsed_s": round(elapsed, 1),
        "frac_boundary_touching": round(bnd, 4),
        "frac_c405_peak_inside": round(peak, 4),
        "frac_tight_bbox_in_pickle": round(in_pickle, 4),
        "median_volume_um3": round(med_vol, 1),
        "median_solidity": round(med_sol, 4),
        "median_sphericity": round(med_sph, 4),
        "median_knn_d1_um": round(med_knn1, 2),
        "median_c405_core_minus_shell_p90": round(med_c405_contrast, 2),
        "median_gfp_feature_value": round(med_gfp, 6),
    }


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Extract per-ROI features for all benchmark subjects.")
    parser.add_argument("--force", action="store_true",
                        help="Re-extract even if cache exists.")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    summary_rows = []
    errors = []

    for sid in SUBJECTS:
        print(f"\n{'='*60}")
        print(f"Subject {sid}")
        print(f"{'='*60}")

        cache_path = _features_cache_path(sid)
        if cache_path.exists() and not args.force:
            print(f"  [CACHE HIT] loading {cache_path}")
            t0 = time.time()
            df = pd.read_parquet(cache_path)
            elapsed = time.time() - t0
            print(f"  loaded {len(df)} ROIs in {elapsed:.1f}s")
        else:
            try:
                s = load_subject(sid)
                t0 = time.time()
                df = extract_roi_features(s, level=2, cache=not args.force)
                elapsed = time.time() - t0
                if elapsed > WALLCLOCK_BUDGET_S:
                    print(f"\n  WARNING: [{sid}] took {elapsed/60:.1f} min > 30 min budget.")
                    print(f"  Features are complete but you may want to review.")
            except Exception as exc:
                import traceback
                print(f"\n  ERROR [{sid}]: {exc}")
                traceback.print_exc()
                errors.append({"sid": sid, "error": str(exc)})
                continue

        row = _summary_row(sid, df, elapsed)
        summary_rows.append(row)
        print(f"\n  Summary for {sid}:")
        for k, v in row.items():
            print(f"    {k}: {v}")

    # ── write summary CSV ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if summary_rows:
        summary_df = pd.DataFrame(summary_rows)
        summary_path = OUTPUTS_DIR / "feature_summary.csv"
        summary_df.to_csv(summary_path, index=False)
        print(f"\n\nSummary written to {summary_path}")
        print(summary_df.to_string(index=False))

    if errors:
        print(f"\n\nERRORS ({len(errors)}):")
        for e in errors:
            print(f"  {e['sid']}: {e['error']}")
        sys.exit(1)

    print(f"\n\nAll {len(summary_rows)} subjects done.")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.text:
So for spot subjects: pickle has 121704 cells, centroids have 127275. The ~5571 extra centroid IDs have no pickle entry. This is fine — for those IDs we fall back to tight-bbox via zarr scan of their small neighborhood (but we can't use the pickle bbox as a crop guide). The `compute_tight_hcr_bboxes` function from `roi_area_sxy.py` skips IDs not in the pickle. 

For the feature extractor, I should use the tight-bbox approach slightly differently: for cells with no pickle entry, I still need tight bboxes. The best approach for all subjects is: use the existing parquet cache where available, and for any missing IDs (whether because they're not in pickle or because the subject has no pickle), scan the zarr around the centroid location.

Let me think about this more carefully. For the full pipeline, I'll use centroid positions to define a crop window for cells without pickle coverage, and for intensity subjects I do the full scan. Let me update `get_tight_bboxes` to handle this:

207	def get_tight_bboxes(s: SubjectData, force: bool = False) -> pd.DataFrame:
208	    """Return tight-bbox DataFrame for all labels in subject s.
209

211	    For intensity subjects (no pickle): scans the full zarr once and caches.
212	    """
213	    sid = s.subject_id
214	    cache_path = _tight_bbox_cache_path(sid)
215	
216	    if cache_path.exists() and not force:
217	        df = pd.read_parquet(cache_path)
218	        n_labels = len(s.hcr_centroids)
219	        print(f"  [{sid}] tight-bbox cache hit: {len(df)} entries "
220	              f"(vs {n_labels} centroids)")
221	        return df
222	
223	    if sid in SPOT_SUBJECTS:
224	        # Spot subjects: must have pickle — use the roi_area_sxy pattern.
225	        # Import lazily to avoid circular deps.
226	        from roi_area_sxy import compute_tight_hcr_bboxes
227	        all_ids = s.hcr_centroids["hcr_id"].astype(int).tolist()

229

content.text:
The `get_tight_bboxes` function for spot subjects will call `compute_tight_hcr_bboxes` with all centroid IDs. But since the pickle only covers 121704 of 127275 centroids for 788406, the remaining 5571 will be silently skipped (the function checks `if m is None: continue`). This is acceptable — those cells without pickle entries won't have intensity stats either, but we should note this. The cache miss is the bigger issue: the current cache for 788406 only has 3185 entries. Calling `compute_tight_hcr_bboxes` with all IDs will add ~121k entries.

However, this could be very slow (121k zarr reads). Let me check how long the existing 3185 took and estimate timing. From the roi_area_sxy.py, reading at ~200 cells/s, 121k cells would take ~600s = 10 min. That's within budget.

But for intensity subjects with 84k cells and a zarr scan — the zarr is 1641 Z slices, 7392×7392 Y×X, at 128 chunks that's 1641/128 ≈ 13 chunks per column × 58×58 ≈ 43k chunks. Scanning could be slow. Let me estimate:

content.text:
Cells are in z=163..1410. Slices 0..162 are empty. I can optimize the scan to start from the centroid bounding box. But let me also consider: since the cells take up z=163..1410 which is ~1248 slices, that's ~10 chunks — and at 35s/chunk that's ~350s. Still under budget.

The bigger optimization is to reduce the per-chunk read by using the x/y centroid bounds too. For 755252 cells: y_px max is 1842 (level-2), so level-0 y_max = 1842*4 = 7368 ≈ 7391. x similar. So the full y/x is needed.

content.text: Now let's also handle the spot subjects properly — `compute_tight_hcr_bboxes` from `roi_area_sxy.py` reads cells from pickle, but for IDs not in pickle it silently skips. For roi_quality we want ALL centroid IDs. Let me add a centroid-based fallback for IDs with no pickle entry:

input.new_string:
if sid in SPOT_SUBJECTS:
        # Spot subjects: use pickle-guided tight bboxes for all pickle IDs,
        # then fall back to centroid-seeded crop for any IDs not in pickle.
        from roi_area_sxy import compute_tight_hcr_bboxes
        all_ids = s.hcr_centroids["hcr_id"].astype(int).tolist()
        df = compute_tight_hcr_bboxes(sid, all_ids, verbose=True)
        covered = set(int(x) for x in df["hcr_id"].values)
        missing_ids = [i for i in all_ids if i not in covered]
        if missing_ids:
            print(f"  [{sid}] {len(missing_ids)} centroids have no pickle entry — "
                  f"computing from centroid-seeded zarr crops")
            extra_df = _compute_bboxes_from_centroids(s, missing_ids)
            if not extra_df.empty:
                combined = pd.concat([df, extra_df], ignore_index=True)
                combined = combined.sort_values("hcr_id").reset_index(drop=True)
                combined.to_parquet(cache_path, index=False)
                df = combined
        return df

content.text: Now add the centroid-seeded crop function:

input.old_string: def get_tight_bboxes(s: SubjectData, force: bool = False) -> pd.DataFrame:
input.new_string:
def _compute_bboxes_from_centroids(
    s: SubjectData,
    hcr_ids: List[int],
    half_window_vox: int = 40,
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Compute tight bboxes for cells with no pickle entry, using centroid position.

    For each hcr_id in hcr_ids, reads a zarr crop of ±half_window_vox voxels
    around the centroid and locates the label.

    Centroids are in level-2 pixel frame; we multiply xy by HCR_SEG_XY_DOWNSAMPLE
    to get level-0 voxel coords.
    """
    if not hcr_ids:
        return pd.DataFrame()

    cent = s.hcr_centroids.set_index("hcr_id")
    seg_zarr = zarr.open(str(_seg_zarr_path(s)), mode="r")["0"]
    _, _, Z, Y, X = seg_zarr.shape
    W = half_window_vox
    new_rows = []

    for hid in tqdm(hcr_ids, desc="centroid-seeded bboxes"):
        if hid not in cent.index:
            continue
        row = cent.loc[hid]
        # level-2 → level-0 voxel coords
        zc = int(round(float(row["z_px"])))
        yc = int(round(float(row["y_px"]) * HCR_SEG_XY_DOWNSAMPLE))
        xc = int(round(float(row["x_px"]) * HCR_SEG_XY_DOWNSAMPLE))
        zlo, zhi = max(0, zc - W), min(Z, zc + W)
        ylo, yhi = max(0, yc - W), min(Y, yc + W)
        xlo, xhi = max(0, xc - W), min(X, xc + W)
        crop = np.asarray(seg_zarr[0, 0, zlo:zhi, ylo:yhi, xlo:xhi])
        mask = (crop == hid)
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        zs, ys, xs = np.where(mask)
        n = int(mask.sum())
        new_rows.append({
            "hcr_id": int(hid),
            "zmin_vox": int(zs.min() + zlo),
            "zmax_vox": int(zs.max() + zlo) + 1,
            "ymin_vox": int(ys.min() + ylo),
            "ymax_vox": int(ys.max() + ylo) + 1,
            "xmin_vox": int(xs.min() + xlo),
            "xmax_vox": int(xs.max() + xlo) + 1,
            "volume_vox": n,
            "zc_vox": float(zs.mean() + zlo),
            "yc_vox": float(ys.mean() + ylo),
            "xc_vox": float(xs.mean() + xlo),
        })

    return pd.DataFrame(new_rows)

content.text:
782149 has 39291 centroids, 35600 in pickle. The cache only has 879. Calling `compute_tight_hcr_bboxes` with 35600 IDs would take ~178s. That's feasible per se, but the full pipeline (all 6 subjects, each with 35k-127k cells) would be slow.

I need to rethink the strategy. The tight-bbox computation for all cells in every subject is the bottleneck. For the feature extractor, there's a simpler approach: for each ROI, we already know the centroid position. For cells with pickle entries, we have a reliable crop window. For cells without pickle or for intensity subjects, we scan the zarr.

However, the task says "per-ROI loop over 100k cells is acceptable" — it just needs to be within 30 min per subject. Let me estimate: 788406 has 121704 cells in pickle, at ~200 cells/s = 608s ≈ 10 min. That's within budget. 

But the current cache only has 3185 entries (the strict GFP+ subset). The remaining 118519 cells need to be computed. That would trigger `compute_tight_hcr_bboxes` for all missing IDs. That single call would take ~10 min.

The approach is sound, but the tight bbox computation for all cells needs to be the first step, and it's a one-time cost that gets cached.     """Return tight-bbox DataFrame for all labels in subject s.

    For spot subjects: uses the existing cache (extended by roi_area_sxy.py).
    For intensity subjects (no pickle): scans the full zarr once and caches.
    """
    sid = s.subject_id
    cache_path = _tight_bbox_cache_path(sid)

        df = pd.read_parquet(cache_path)
        n_labels = len(s.hcr_centroids)
        print(f"  [{sid}] tight-bbox cache hit: {len(df)} entries "
              f"(vs {n_labels} centroids)")
        return df

    if sid in SPOT_SUBJECTS:
        # Spot subjects: use pickle-guided tight bboxes for all pickle IDs,
        # then fall back to centroid-seeded crop for any IDs not in pickle.
        from roi_area_sxy import compute_tight_hcr_bboxes
        all_ids = s.hcr_centroids["hcr_id"].astype(int).tolist()
        df = compute_tight_hcr_bboxes(sid, all_ids, verbose=True)
        covered = set(int(x) for x in df["hcr_id"].values)
        missing_ids = [i for i in all_ids if i not in covered]

            extra_df = _compute_bboxes_from_centroids(s, missing_ids)
            if not extra_df.empty:
                combined = pd.concat([df, extra_df], ignore_index=True)
                combined = combined.sort_values("hcr_id").reset_index(drop=True)

def get_tight_bboxes(s: SubjectData, force: bool = False) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Return tight-bbox DataFrame for all centroid IDs in subject s.

    For spot subjects: uses pickle-guided tight bboxes (via roi_area_sxy cache),
    extending the cache if needed.  Falls back to centroid-seeded crops for
    cells without a pickle entry.
    For intensity subjects: scans the full zarr once and caches.

    The cache is keyed by subject ID.  If the cache exists but covers fewer
    IDs than the centroid set, missing IDs are computed and the cache is extended.
    """
    sid = s.subject_id
    cache_path = _tight_bbox_cache_path(sid)
    all_ids_set = set(s.hcr_centroids["hcr_id"].astype(int).tolist())

    # Load existing cache if present
    existing_df = None
    if cache_path.exists() and not force:
        existing_df = pd.read_parquet(cache_path)
        covered = set(int(x) for x in existing_df["hcr_id"].values)
        if covered >= all_ids_set:
            print(f"  [{sid}] tight-bbox cache FULL: {len(existing_df)} entries")
            return existing_df[existing_df["hcr_id"].isin(all_ids_set)].reset_index(drop=True)
        missing_ids = sorted(all_ids_set - covered)
        print(f"  [{sid}] tight-bbox cache PARTIAL: {len(covered)} cached, "
              f"{len(missing_ids)} to compute")
    else:
        missing_ids = sorted(all_ids_set)
        covered = set()

    if sid in INTENSITY_SUBJECTS:
        # No pickle — scan the full zarr.
        print(f"  [{sid}] no metrics.pickle — scanning full seg zarr (one-time)...")
        seg_zarr = zarr.open(str(_seg_zarr_path(s)), mode="r")["0"]
        print(f"  [{sid}] seg shape: {seg_zarr.shape}, dtype: {seg_zarr.dtype}")
        t0 = time.time()
        Z_total = seg_zarr.shape[2]
        z_lo_scan = 0
        z_hi_scan = Z_total
        if not s.hcr_centroids.empty:
            z_lo_scan = max(0, int(s.hcr_centroids["z_px"].min()) - 5)
            z_hi_scan = min(Z_total, int(s.hcr_centroids["z_px"].max()) + 5)
        print(f"  [{sid}] scanning z=[{z_lo_scan}, {z_hi_scan}) of {Z_total}")
        df = _scan_seg_zarr_for_bboxes(
            seg_zarr, chunk_z=128, verbose=True, z_lo=z_lo_scan, z_hi=z_hi_scan
        )
        elapsed = time.time() - t0
        print(f"  [{sid}] scan done: {len(df)} labels in {elapsed:.1f}s")
        df = df.sort_values("hcr_id").reset_index(drop=True)
        df.to_parquet(cache_path, index=False)
        print(f"  [{sid}] tight-bbox saved → {cache_path}")
        return df

    # Spot subjects: use pickle-guided bboxes for pickle cells
    from roi_area_sxy import compute_tight_hcr_bboxes
    pickle_path = _metrics_path(s)
    if pickle_path is not None:
        with open(pickle_path, "rb") as f:
            metrics_keys = set(int(k) for k in pickle.load(f).keys())
    else:
        metrics_keys = set()

    pickle_missing = [i for i in missing_ids if i in metrics_keys]
    no_pickle_ids = [i for i in missing_ids if i not in metrics_keys]

    new_df_parts = []
    if existing_df is not None and not existing_df.empty:
        new_df_parts.append(existing_df)

    if pickle_missing:
        print(f"  [{sid}] computing {len(pickle_missing)} cells via pickle+zarr crops...")
        part = compute_tight_hcr_bboxes(sid, pickle_missing, verbose=True)
        if not part.empty:
            new_df_parts.append(part)

    if no_pickle_ids:
        print(f"  [{sid}] {len(no_pickle_ids)} cells have no pickle entry — "
              f"computing from centroid-seeded zarr crops")
        part = _compute_bboxes_from_centroids(s, no_pickle_ids)
        if not part.empty:
            new_df_parts.append(part)

    if not new_df_parts:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=["hcr_id", "zmin_vox", "ymin_vox", "xmin_vox",
                                      "zmax_vox", "ymax_vox", "xmax_vox",
                                      "volume_vox", "zc_vox", "yc_vox", "xc_vox"])

    combined = pd.concat(new_df_parts, ignore_index=True)
    combined = combined.drop_duplicates("hcr_id", keep="last")
    combined = combined.sort_values("hcr_id").reset_index(drop=True)
    combined.to_parquet(cache_path, index=False)
    print(f"  [{sid}] tight-bbox cache updated: {len(combined)} total entries → {cache_path}")
    return combined[combined["hcr_id"].isin(all_ids_set)].reset_index(drop=True)
